section_title: INTRODUCTION TO CLASSIC SWING TRADING APPROACH
content:
We are reviewing Lesson Three for the February 2017 ICT Mentorship content, specifically dealing with the
classic swing trading approach.

section_title: DEFINING MARKET CONDITIONS AND DIRECTION
content:
When defining market conditions, we think in terms of where the price can reach for, both in advance and
decline. This is the foundation for determining likely market direction.

section_title: PD ARRAYS AND IMPLIED MARKET DIRECTION
content:
The PD arrays that have been most recently traded to or executed on indicate that the opposite PD array
Spectrum will be reached for.

section_title: PROBABILITY SHIFTS BASED ON PD ARRAY INTERACTION
content:
- If discount arrays have provided support for price, probabilities increase that the premium arrays will be
sought above the market price.
- If premium arrays have provided resistance for price, probabilities increase that discount arrays will be
sought below the market price.

section_title: MARKET PRICE AND CHART REFERENCE EXAMPLE
content:
Assume this is the market price, which could be in any asset class. For example, we're looking at the Swiss
franc on a monthly chart.

section_title: IDENTIFYING NEAREST MITIGATION BLOCKS
content:
We look for the nearest bearish mitigation block above us and the nearest bullish mitigation block below us.
These arrays may not exist in current price action, but this is the spectrum to look through above and below
the marketplace in this order.

section_title: IDENTIFYING BREAKER BLOCKS
content:
- Above you, look for any bearish Breakers that have not yet been traded up to.
- Below you, look for any bullish Breakers that have not been traded to.

section_title: IDENTIFYING LIQUIDITY VOIDS (BEARISH)
content:
Above you, look for liquidity voids where price is quickly drawn lower or repriced aggressively fast, leaving a
big range of only downside delivery.

section_title: IDENTIFYING LIQUIDITY VOIDS (BULLISH)
content:
Conversely, look for any liquidity voids below you where price has shown a strong willingness to rally
quickly, leaving a porous wake of only buy side delivery in price, in other words, big ranges up.

section_title: IDENTIFYING FAIR VALUE GAPS AND ORDER BLOCKS
content:
- Above current market price, look for any fair value gaps.
- Below you, look for any fair value gaps.
- Look for the nearest bearish order block above you.
- Below you, look for the nearest bullish order block.

section_title: IDENTIFYING REJECTION BLOCKS (HIGHS)
content:
Look for candles that have Wicks at short-term highs. For any candle that has Wicks above it, note the
bodies of the candle because there would be liquidity resting just above the bodies of the candle. These
are rejection blocks.

section_title: IDENTIFYING REJECTION BLOCKS (LOWS)
content:
Below the candles that have Wicks, you would look at the candles' bodies, and below it, there would be
liquidity. These would be rejection blocks respectively.

section_title: IDENTIFYING OLD HIGHS AND LOWS
content:
Look at the old high or any historical low that are below, and look for any old low or any historical high that
are above.

section_title: FRAMING PD ARRAYS INTO PREMIUM AND DISCOUNT SPECTRUM
content:
Framing the PD arrays above market price is in the premium Spectrum, and the arrays below market price
is the discount Spectrum.

section_title: MULTI-TIMEFRAME APPLICATION OF PD ARRAYS
content: You will be looking at the monthly, weekly, daily, and four-hour charts the same way as outlined here.

section_title: REINFORCING THE PD ARRAY MATRIX
content:
When referring to determining the premium arrays and discount arrays, this is what we are referring to. This
is the PD array Matrix:
- Every array above market price is the premium Spectrum.
- Every array below current market action is the discount Spectrum.

section_title: IDENTIFYING DISPLACEMENT IN MARKET PRICE
content:
You are looking for which side of the marketplace has most recently shown a displacement. Has price
moved away from price levels aggressively? Was there speed involved?

section_title: SMART MONEY INFLUENCE AND DIRECTIONAL PROBABILITY
content:
Were there large ranges indicating a massive push by smart money? We understand that this entity has
more money than us collectively, and it's their business. If they push price around on these higher time
frame charts, there is a great deal of probability that that direction is most likely going to be at least
tradable in the same direction.

section_title: HIGHER TIME FRAME SEQUENCE FOR SHORTING
content:
- INTRODUCTION TO SELL
PROGRAMS
We're looking at shorting opportunities and referring to sell and buy programs. A sell program first starts
with a monthly chart.

section_title: MONTHLY CHART SELL PROGRAM INITIATION
content:
We look for the market to move away from a resistance level. This could be:
- A bearish order block.
- Trading away from an old high.
- Trading away from a historical low acting as resistance.
When that time frame is indicating that it wants to go lower, that is a sell program.

section_title: WEEKLY CHART SELL PROGRAM CONFIRMATION
content:
We look for the same thing to occur in the weekly chart. We look for premium arrays in the weekly chart to
indicate that price wants to reach for the discount arrays below us. This creates a sell program on the
weekly chart.

section_title: DAILY CHART SELL PROGRAM CONFIRMATION
content:
This continues into the daily chart. You would look for premium arrays to cause a sell program or bearish
prices for the daily.

section_title: FOUR HOUR CHART EXECUTION FOR SHORTING
content:
For the executable time frame for swing trading, the four-hour chart, you would look for the same thing. You
would look for premium arrays to cause price to reject or resist going higher and expand lower, seeking
discount Spectrum PD arrays.

section_title: IDEAL SHORTING CONDITIONS
content:
- TIMEFRAME ALIGNMENT
For ideal shorting conditions and opportunities, we look for the monthly, weekly, daily, and four-hour charts
to be in alignment, with price moving away from premium PD arrays.

section_title: HIGHER TIME FRAME SEQUENCE FOR BUYING
content:
- INTRODUCTION TO BUY PROGRAMS
The opposite is true for buying opportunities. We look for the monthly chart to be in a buy program,
meaning it's moving away from discount arrays.

section_title: MONTHLY CHART BUY PROGRAM INITIATION
content:
We look for the monthly chart to be seeking to move higher, to reach up into premium arrays. This would be
a buy program on the monthly chart.

section_title: WEEKLY CHART BUY PROGRAM CONFIRMATION
content:
On the weekly chart, you will be looking for price finding sensitivity at discount arrays, moving price into a
buy program. You would expect to see the weekly chart moving higher or trading higher.

section_title: DAILY CHART BUY PROGRAM CONFIRMATION
content:
The daily chart also, when it's finding support at discount arrays, we would expect to see price moving
higher on the daily chart.

section_title: FOUR HOUR CHART EXECUTION AND TIMEFRAME SYNCHRONIZATION FOR BUYING
content:
On the four-hour executable time frame, we would be looking for sensitivity in the discount arrays for buying
opportunities. This way, we get in sync with the monthly, weekly, daily, and four-hour charts all being in sync
for bullish or buy program opportunities.

section_title: HIGHEST PROBABILITY SWING TRADING SETUPS
content:
When swing trading, the easiest and most probable directional setups are when all monthly, weekly, daily,
and four-hour charts are in alignment.

section_title: BULLISH ALIGNMENT EXAMPLE
content:
When we have the monthly, weekly, and daily charts all bullish, and the four-hour trades down into a
discount and finds support (for instance, at a bullish order block), that is a high probability condition.

section_title: WHY BULLISH ALIGNMENT CREATES HIGH PROBABILITY
content:
This is a high probability condition because you have the monthly, weekly, and daily in sync with you, and
it's already an existing up market. So, its bullish undertones are going to be supporting the idea on a four
- hour chart.

section_title: BULLISH UNDERTONES SUPPORTING FOUR-HOUR BUY SETUPS (1)
content:
If you're looking for bullish order blocks or buying below an old low (scooping up sell stops just like a
market maker would), the bullish undertones of the higher timeframes will support this idea on a four-hour
chart.

section_title: BULLISH UNDERTONES SUPPORTING FOUR-HOUR BUY SETUPS (2)
content:
Trading back down into a liquidity void, for instance, like an optimal trade entry for a fair value trade going
back into a fair value Gap, these ideas are also supported by the bullish higher timeframe undertones.

section_title: HIGH PROBABILITY BUY OPPORTUNITIES FROM TIMEFRAME AGREEMENT
content:
All these ideas are supported with the notion that monthly, weekly, and daily are already bullish, and
therefore the four-hour should be in agreement with it. Your buying opportunity should be high probability.

section_title: HIGH PROBABILITY BEARISH OPPORTUNITIES
content:
- TIMEFRAME AGREEMENT
The reverse is true for bearish opportunities when the monthly, weekly, and daily are moving lower and are
in sell programs.

section_title: FOUR-HOUR PREMIUM PD ARRAY ENTRIES FOR SHORTING
content:
When you see the four-hour chart go up into premium PD arrays (bearish order blocks, bearish liquidity
voids, bearish fair value gaps, bearish Breakers, or trading above an old high to run out buy stops), these
are conditions for shorting.

section_title: HIGHER TIME FRAME SUPPORT AND PROBABILITY SHIFT FOR SHORTING
content:
Those conditions are high probability because you have the monthly, weekly, and daily in sync looking for
lower prices. These higher time frames are going to draw heavily on that lower time frame four-hour chart,
so those probabilities shift to a great deal of favor for you as the trader that wants to go short in those
conditions.

section_title: RESOURCES AND DECISION POINT MANAGEMENT
content:
PDF files will be provided in August that outline different scenarios in a flowchart format. Certain conditions
will lead you to certain decision points, and once you get to those decision points, you have to wait for price
to either confirm or negate the opportunity.

section_title: CLASSIC TRADING APPROACH MODEL
content:
- HIGHER TIMEFRAME BIAS
For this simple classic trading approach model, the market is already predisposed or poised to trade higher
on a higher time frame.

section_title: HIGHER TIMEFRAME ANALYSIS
content:
- SEASONAL AND INTEREST RATE FACTORS
This higher timeframe bias can be determined by analysis, including:
- Seasonal interest rate driven factors (always involved in your trading).
- Commitment of Traders (COT) report data.

section_title: HIGHER TIMEFRAME ANALYSIS
content:
- COMMITMENT OF TRADERS (COT)
Look at large commercial traders: Are they net long on Swiss franc? If their hedging program has indicated
long-term buying, and every time it goes to a net long position or if they aggressively lessen their short
positions on the COT report, that would be supportive of bullish prices for Swiss franc.

section_title: COT LINE GRAPH AND INTER-MARKET ANALYSIS FOR BULLISHNESS
content:
When the COT line graph indicates such conditions, it would be supportive of bullish prices for Swiss franc.
Additionally, inter-market analysis, such as SMT Divergence or Market structure, can support this
bullishness.

section_title: MACRO SCALE CONFIRMATION FOR HIGHER TIMEFRAME DIRECTION
content:
You just need a few things on a macro scale to support the idea that on a higher time frame, prices should
be trading higher. You don't need everything in agreement; just a small sample size of a few things
supposing that the higher time frame direction should be bullish.

section_title: HIGH PROBABILITY FACTORS
content:
- INTEREST RATES
When you have a few things in your favor indicating a bullish bias, interest rates are often the highest
probability factor in your favor.

section_title: INTEREST RATE DIFFERENTIAL EXAMPLE
content:
- NZD AND AUD
Consider the interest rate differential, as discussed in the mentorship. The New Zealand dollar and the
Australian dollar have the highest interest rates among the currencies traded in Forex.

section_title: RATIONALE FOR BUYING HIGHER YIELD CURRENCIES
content:
The differential between these currencies and others means they have higher interest rates. Therefore, the
yield will be higher on those countries and their currencies, making people more likely to buy them than to
sell them, due to the available interest rate differential.

section_title: ASSUMING HIGHER TIMEFRAME FUNDAMENTAL BIAS
content:
Let's assume we've already arrived at a combination of conditions that you're using to employ with your
higher time frame.

section_title: FUNDAMENTAL OUTLOOK AND INTER-MARKET ANALYSIS FOR BULLISHNESS
content:
Your fundamental outlook on price on a higher time frame, and it's in agreement with a measure of inter
- market analysis, is supporting that bullishness.

section_title: WAITING FOR RETRACEMENT AFTER A MARKET RALLY
content:
The market will rally higher, and then we are waiting for a retracement. We must be patient and wait for the
retracement to come to fruition.

section_title: LONG-TERM BULLISHNESS
content:
Once that retracement occurs, then we have the stage for the setup to occur. In other words, we have a
condition in the marketplace that outlines bullishness long term.

section_title: HIGHEST PROBABILITY DIRECTION FOR SWING TRADING
content:
We wait for markets with these conditions to rally. This is the highest probable direction type of trading for
swing trading. When the market rallies or shows displacement as an impulse price swing, it moves away
from some specific price level.

section_title: ANTICIPATING SUPPORT AND FALSE BREAKS FOR BUYS
content:
This could be a level where we were anticipating support, or we were waiting for an old low not to slide
further in price. Perhaps we are looking for a 'turtle soup buy,' which is a false break below an old low, but
we don't want to buy it yet.

section_title: WAITING FOR CONFIRMATION AND CONFIDENCE IN BUY SETUPS
content:
We haven't built up our confidence yet or are not as astute in price action. So, we wait and see if price will
give us clues and confidence that it has made a low, ran out stops, and then starts to go higher.

section_title: RALLY, RETRACEMENT, AND SETUP
content:
When a rally occurs, we simply wait for a retracement. Once that retracement occurs, then you have a
stage at which the market will be setting up a condition that permits you to take a trade entry.

section_title: THE THREE PHASES OF A TRADE
content:
There is:
- A condition first.
- Then there's the stage portion of taking the trade.
- Then there's the execution, when the market expands up to higher prices. That's what you're trying to
profit on.

section_title: NORMAL PROCEDURE
content:
- PREDISPOSED MARKET AND MACRO IDEAS
The normal procedure is that you're looking for a market that's predisposed to go higher. You have higher
time frame macro ideas behind the notion that markets should go higher.

section_title: MARKET RALLY, DISPLACEMENT, IMPULSE SWING, AND RETRACEMENT
content:
The market has to rally first in order to have displacement. The market creates an Impulse price swing.
When that impulse price swing occurs, we wait for the retracement.
EXAMPLE:

section_title: SWISS FRANC RALLY FROM POINT OF ORIGIN
content:
For instance, we're looking at the marketplace and we think that Swiss franc should be rallying from a
specific point of origin.

section_title: IDENTIFYING DISPLACEMENT AND IMPULSE PRICE SWING
content:
When we see price move higher, we know that there is a displacement there, so we have an Impulse price
swing.

section_title: AVOIDING PREMATURE ENTRY DURING A RALLY
content:
During this entire rally, the public or less informed, reactionary traders will be 'dog piling' on this. We do not
do that; we wait.

section_title: WAITING FOR RETRACEMENT FROM PREMIUM ARRAYS
content:
We look for conditions in the marketplace, while it's rallying up, where it could offer resistance. This will be
in the form of premium arrays, such as:
- Bearish order block.
- Breaker.
- Mitigation block.
- Void.
- Fair value Gap.
- Old high or old low.
We anticipate the market to start topping out and eventually give us a retracement.

section_title: RETRACEMENT AS A POTENTIAL COUNTER-TREND OPPORTUNITY
content:
We are not trying to time the retracement, but during this small retracement back (if this is a monthly or
weekly chart), that 'red area' of retracement could be a counter-trend opportunity.

section_title: FUTURE CONTENT ON COUNTER-TREND SWING TRADING
content:
While not being taught for swing trading here, ideas for counter-trend swing trades will be given in August
during the PDF portion of the mentorship. These would basically occur during the periods shown
graphically in the move in red, where you would be taking shorts.

section_title: FOCUS ON HIGH PROBABILITY LONG ENTRIES DURING RETRACEMENT
content:
We are looking primarily for high probability long entries. When the retracement occurs, we're looking for
specific price levels at which we would expect and anticipate sensitivity for bullish buy opportunities.

section_title: HUNTING FOR LONG-TERM BULLISH ALIGNMENT
content:
During that retracement, we are 'hunting,' looking for things that line up for us to get in sync with the long
- term analysis that we've arrived at, believing that the higher time frame is going to be moving up or bullish
from where we are now.

section_title: ANTICIPATING THE EXPANSION SWING
content:
Eventually, we are anticipating this portion to be the expansion swing. This portion is where price reaches
for some higher time frame resistance level of some sort.

section_title: DEFINING THE RANGE FOR DISCOUNT PD ARRAY MATRIX
content:
We define our range by our point of origin and the intermediate term high prior to the retracement. Once
these two markers are identified, you define that range in terms of discount to premium.

section_title: CONSTRUCTING THE DISCOUNT PD ARRAY MATRIX WITHIN THE RANGE
content:
Everything below that high down to the point of origin will be referred to as your discount PD array Matrix.
Inside that range, you're going to be looking for all the things we previously mapped out for the discount
Matrix.

section_title: KEY DISCOUNT PD ARRAY ELEMENTS FOR BUYING (1)
content:
First, you would look for:
- Any mitigation blocks.
- Any bullish Breakers.
- Any liquidity voids.
- Any fair value gaps.
- A bullish order block.
- Any candles that have long Wicks, looking for the market to seek liquidity below the bodies of those
candles.

section_title: LOCATING DISCOUNT PD ARRAYS WITHIN THE IMPULSE SWING
content:
All those ideas are looked for inside of the impulse swing, from the point of origin all the way through the
impulse swing that makes that intermediate term high, right before it retraces. Inside the impulse swing,
we're looking for all those PD arrays on a discount basis.

section_title: IDENTIFYING INSTITUTIONAL ORDER FLOW ENTRY POINTS
content:
We're looking to map out what levels would be indicative of having a strong likelihood to buy, or where we
would see institutional order flow step in and start sending price higher again.

section_title: COMPLETING THE DISCOUNT ARRAY MATRIX MAPPING
content:
Once you do this, you've already outlined what you're looking for. You'll know what price levels you're
looking for, and that's how you map out your discount array Matrix.

section_title: SWING TRADE PROCEDURE
content:
- MONTHLY AND WEEKLY MARKET PROFILE (9-18
MONTHS)
On the monthly and weekly charts, you're looking for the 9 to 18 month market profile: Are we in a trending
environment or are we in a range-bound consolidation?

section_title: IDENTIFYING AGGRESSIVE MOVEMENT FROM CONSOLIDATION
content:
If we're in a consolidation, you're looking for a market that leaves a consolidation aggressively. In other
words, it moves out of a small trading range and moves aggressively higher.

section_title: FOLLOWING DISPLACEMENT FOR TRENDING OPPORTUNITIES
content:
On a monthly or weekly chart, that shows there's displacement. We have to start following that marketplace
because it's most likely going to want to trend, and we have to be looking for those opportunities to come to
fruition.

section_title: DETERMINING MONTHLY PD ARRAYS FOR BULLISH MARKETS
content:
Determine the monthly PD arrays. Since we're looking at bullish markets, we're going to refer to the active
discount arrays.

section_title: IDENTIFYING ACTIVE MONTHLY DISCOUNT ARRAYS
content:
This involves looking for:
- Bullish order blocks.
- Liquidity voids below the marketplace.
- Fair value gaps underneath the market price.
- Old lows.
- Bullish Breakers.
- Bullish mitigation blocks.

section_title: DETERMINING MONTHLY PREMIUM ARRAYS AFTER DISCOUNT SUPPORT
content:
If the market shows the monthly discount array supporting price, you then determine where the premium
arrays are on the monthly chart.

section_title: TRANSPOSING MONTHLY ACTIVE DISCOUNT LEVELS TO WEEKLY CHART
content:
When monthly discount arrays are active (meaning you know which ones they are and you're 'hunting'),
you move down into the weekly chart and transpose those monthly levels over to your weekly chart.

section_title: DETERMINING WEEKLY PD ARRAYS AND ACTIVE DISCOUNT ARRAYS
content:
Then, you're going to determine your weekly PD arrays and refer specifically to the active discount arrays
on your weekly chart. If weekly discount arrays are supporting price, determine the premium arrays on the
weekly chart as well.

section_title: ACTIVE WEEKLY DISCOUNT ARRAY ELEMENTS AND TRANSPOSING TO DAILY
content:
When the weekly discount arrays are active (meaning you've already defined them, such as bullish order
blocks, bullish liquidity voids, bullish Breakers, bullish mitigation blocks, old lows, fair value gaps below the
marketplace), once you arrive at them, transpose those to your daily chart.

section_title: DETERMINING DAILY DISCOUNT ARRAYS
content:
Then, you're going to determine your daily discount arrays and refer to the active discount arrays on your
daily, defining each on that daily time frame.

section_title: TRANSPOSING ACTIVE DAILY DISCOUNT ARRAYS TO FOUR-HOUR CHART
content:
Once you understand where the daily discount arrays are active (meaning you know which ones are there,
as there may not be a void or a breaker), you take whichever ones are active in the discount Spectrum and
transpose those over to a four-hour chart.

section_title: DETERMINING FOUR-HOUR DISCOUNT ARRAYS
content: Then, you're going to determine your four-hour discount arrays, labeling which ones are in the discount.

section_title: IDENTIFYING ACTIVE FOUR-HOUR DISCOUNT ARRAYS
content:
They may not be Breakers, voids, or gaps, but there are old lows and bullish order blocks, and those need
to be defined. You're going to be referring to the active discount arrays on that time frame as well.

section_title: EXECUTING BUYS ON FOUR-HOUR DISCOUNT ARRAYS (1)
content:
You're going to buy all four-hour discount arrays. This includes:
- Every bullish order block.
- Every bullish Breaker.
- Every mitigation block that's bullish.
- Every old low that's violated, looking for a buying opportunity that could be a 'turtle soup' long entry.

section_title: EXECUTING BUYS ON FOUR-HOUR DISCOUNT ARRAYS (2)
content:
For every rejection block below candles that have Wicks, we look for price to go just below the bodies of
the candles and trade right below that like a 'turtle soup'; it doesn't need to go through the Wicks.

section_title: SCALING OUT PROFITS FROM LONG ENTRIES
content:
When we take these long entries, we look to scale out at daily, weekly, and monthly premium arrays that
have already been defined and transposed to our four-hour chart.

section_title: IDEAL RISK-TO-REWARD RATIO FOR SETUPS
content:
The ideal scenario is to look for trade setups that offer at least three, and preferably more, times the range
between your entry and the closest premium array from all time frames.

section_title: AIMING FOR HIGHER TIMEFRAME TARGETS (LONG)
content:
If you're taking an entry on a four-hour chart, and the monthly chart has the range to go higher and is in a
buy program, you're really trying to aim for that monthly level.

section_title: STRATEGIC PROFIT TAKING AND POSITION SIZING (LONG)
content:
However, you don't hold everything for that monthly level. You're going to take some profits off at the
premium arrays on the four-hour chart, and at the daily chart, and at the weekly chart. As you get closer
and closer to that premium range on the monthly and weekly, you want to have very small portions of your
position on and have already taken profits along the way.

section_title: INITIAL LOSS MANAGEMENT
content:
If a buy setup on the four-hour discount array fails and the trade results in a loss, do not worry, freak out, or
panic. Simply look for a lower-priced four-hour discount array to buy at.

section_title: REFERRING TO HIGHER TIMEFRAME DISCOUNT ARRAYS ON 4H FAILURE
content:
If the four-hour chart has no more discount arrays to buy at, refer to the daily, weekly, and monthly discount
arrays to buy at a lower price.

section_title: POSITION SIZING AND MANAGING LOSS RECOVERY (BULLISH)
content:
At the lower discount array, you're going to buy with 50 of the position size of the initial trade or the
previous failed trade that you just utilized. Do not try to win all of the lost equity back in the subsequent
trade; it may take a few trades to get back that loss. Do not rush, do not force it.

section_title: CONFIRMING PRICE ACTION DURING FAVORABLE MOVEMENT (BULLISH)
content:
Always confirm that lower time frame four-hour premium arrays give way or fail as price moves in your
favor, and new discount arrays support price as it moves toward the premium arrays on the higher time
frame.

section_title: OBSERVING BEARISH PREMIUM ARRAYS GIVING WAY (BULLISH CONFIRMATION)
content:
You're going to be looking for:
- Bearish Breakers to give way.
- Bearish mitigation blocks to give way; they shouldn't hold price back, you should just be going right
through those.
- Going through up candles, which would be noted as potential bearish order blocks.

section_title: EXPANDING THROUGH OLD HIGHS
content:
- INSTITUTIONAL SPONSORSHIP CONFIRMATION
You are not going through old highs and coming back with deep retracements; you're going through old
highs and expanding higher. That is a clear indication you have a strong trade on your side and you have
institutional order flow behind you.

section_title: SWING TRADE PROGRESSION
content:
- MONTHLY CHART
Let's take a look at the swing trade progression. For instance, on a monthly chart, you observe a first
impulse swing, then a retracement.

section_title: CLASSIC TEXTBOOK BULLISH TREND PATTERN
content:
If you're in a bullish market environment, you would expect another expansion price swing, then a
retracement, and another impulse price swing, then a retracement, and another expansion swing, all along
making higher highs and higher lows. That's a normal classic textbook trend that would be deemed bullish
by anyone's standards.

section_title: SHIFTING FOCUS TO WEEKLY CHART EXAMPLE FOR FRACTAL ANALYSIS
content:
Let's refer to this as a condition that's in our weekly chart, though this could also be done on a monthly
timeframe.

section_title: FRACTAL NATURE
content:
- WEEKLY IMPULSE SWING CONTAINING DAILY SWINGS
If we look at the first impulse swing on the left side of the chart, inside that weekly impulse swing, our daily
chart may have a smaller impulse swing with a deep retracement and another secondary expansion swing.

section_title: DEEPER LOOK INTO WEEKLY IMPULSE SWINGS (DAILY STAGES)
content:
You won't always see these stages on the weekly chart, but if you break the weekly chart impulse swing
down and look deeper, you'll sometimes see two stages in the daily chart.

section_title: FRACTAL NATURE
content:
- DAILY EXPANSION SWING CONTAINING SMALLER SWINGS
The expansion swing that occurs later in this example, inside that expansion swing, the same thing is seen.
If this is a daily chart, looking inside that expansion swing, because price is fractal, you would see
subsequently smaller price swings: impulse swing, retracement, expansion swing.

section_title: FRACTAL NATURE
content:
- WEEKLY IMPULSE SWING TO DAILY SWINGS (RIGHT SIDE)
The impulse swing that occurs on the right side of our charts, inside that impulse swing (if this was a
weekly chart), that would be a daily chart that could have an impulse swing, smaller daily retracement, and
then an expansive price swing.

section_title: FRACTAL NATURE
content:
- LARGER EXPANSION SWING CONTAINING SMALLER SWINGS
Then, the second or last expansion swing on a larger price move, inside that expansion swing, there will be
smaller impulse swings with retracements and a lower time frame expansion swing. So, everything in price
is fractal.

section_title: FRACTAL NATURE
content:
- DAILY IMPULSE SWING TO SMALLER SWINGS
The very first impulse swing on the daily chart would have a smaller impulse swing, retracement, and
expansion.

section_title: FRACTAL NATURE
content:
- DAILY EXPANSION SWING TO FOUR-HOUR SWINGS
Then the daily chart's expansion price swing would have in itself a smaller impulse swing, retracement, and
expansion, and that would be seen on the four-hour chart.

section_title: MULTI-TIMEFRAME FRACTAL BREAKDOWN (WEEKLY TO FOUR-HOUR)
content:
The larger impulse swing initially on the left-hand side could represent the weekly range or weekly chart's
impulse swing. A smaller range inside of that weekly could be seen with a daily impulse swing,
retracement, and expansion. Then, each individual price swing higher up on the daily would have four-hour
impulse swings, retracements, and expansions.

section_title: UBIQUITY OF FRACTAL PRICE SWINGS ACROSS ALL TIMEFRAMES
content: This is seen for every price swing along the spectrum from monthly, weekly, daily, and four-hour charts.

section_title: FAILURE PROTOCOL WITHIN FRACTAL PRICE SWINGS (LONG)
content:
If you're buying at any one of these and they fail, you just simply drop down into the next higher time frame
discount array. You would then look for ideas inside of that to be a buyer to be in sync with the larger
monthly and/or weekly macro uptrend.

section_title: INTEGRATING FRACTAL ANALYSIS WITH PD ARRAYS
content:
We look at price on a fractal basis, but we also look at it in terms of defining it in terms of PD arrays, in
other words, premium to discount.

section_title: ALGORITHM SEEKING LIQUIDITY IN DISCOUNT ARRAYS
content:
When price pulls back and the algorithm gets back into these discount arrays, it will look to seek that
liquidity below the market price to allow traders at the bank level to populate those levels with orders, and
then the market will take off.

section_title: GENERAL CONCEPT FOR BEARISH MARKETS
content:
- HIGHER TIMEFRAME
PREDISPOSITION
For bearish markets, the general concept is that we have a market poised or predisposed to trade lower on
a higher time frame. The same defining terms used for bullish market conditions apply here.

section_title: SEASONAL TENDENCY FOR BEARISH PRICE ACTIVITY
content:
It may be a seasonal tendency that supposes the market should be bearish, or that the asset class you're
trading should be having some bearish price activity seasonally, long term, and consistently showing that it
repeats a lot more than it trades higher.

section_title: SEASONAL TENDENCY AS A SUPPORTING IDEA
content:
That seasonal tendency should have an influence on price. It's not a standalone factor, but we use it as a
supporting idea or as a roadmap.

section_title: COMMITMENT OF TRADERS (COT) FOR BEARISHNESS
content:
Commitment of Traders (COT) commercial positions may be heavily net short, indicating a continuation of
bearish prices. You can observe on the weekly chart how they have worked price lower, pulling price
heavily lower in a downtrend every time they went net short, hedging over the last six or nine months.

section_title: CONVERGING FACTORS FOR BEARISHNESS
content:
If your trading market has commercial traders being heavily net short, and you have seasonal tendencies
on your side, and interest rates on your side drawing price lower, then the Commitment of Traders Report
with a heavily net short position by the commercials creates a strong bearish confluence.

section_title: INTER-MARKET ANALYSIS FOR BEARISHNESS
content: Inter-market analysis also supports the idea, including institutional market structure and SMT Divergence.

section_title: MARKET DECLINE, IMPULSE PRICE SWING, AND DISPLACEMENT
content:
The market will have a decline lower, moving away from a point of origin. We may have expected this move,
or we may be surprised by it. We look for displacement; that first price swing is the impulse price swing.
That's what's drawing price away from a level that we either expected to see bearishness, or if we're
surprised by something we didn't expect.

section_title: OBSERVING RETRACEMENT AND HIGHER TIMEFRAME CHANGES
content:
Now we have to analyze what's happening. Is there going to be a retracement? The market goes into the
retracement, and if we look at the higher time frame, something might change that we didn't notice before.

section_title: ANTICIPATING EXPANSION SWING FOR PROFIT (BEARISH)
content:
Or if it's a pair that just caught our eye, we are seeing the impulse price swing and now the retracement.
We're expecting or anticipating the expansion price swing; that's where we look to profit.

section_title: STUDYING PRICE AND DEFINING PD ARRAYS DURING RETRACEMENT (BEARISH)
content:
We have to go into the marketplace during that retracement. We're going to be studying price and defining
it in terms of PD arrays.

section_title: DEFINING THE PREMIUM PD ARRAY MATRIX (BEARISH)
content:
We go back to the point of origin and the short-term low where price bounced, causing that retracement to
come to fruition. We then take the low at the short-term low and all the way up to the point of origin and
define that in terms of the Premium PD array Matrix.

section_title: KEY ELEMENTS OF THE PREMIUM PD ARRAY MATRIX FOR SELLING
content:
This is where we identify all the:
- Bearish order blocks.
- Bearish liquidity voids.
- Bearish Breakers.
- Bearish mitigation blocks.
- Fair value gaps.
- Old highs to sell above.
- Rejection blocks (candles that have long Wicks), where we look to sell just above the bodies of the
candles when we're bearish.

section_title: MULTI-TIMEFRAME PREMIUM ARRAY ANALYSIS AND TRANSPOSITION
content:
We go into the marketplace and do this on a monthly, weekly, daily, and four-hour chart. We define and
determine all of the premium arrays for all those time frames and transpose them over to the lower time
frame.

section_title: MONTHS) FOR BEARISHNESS
content:
You're going to determine the 9 to 18 month market profile. Preferably, you want to see price moving out of
a consolidation, not being in a range. You want to see it wanting to trend.

section_title: HIGHER PROBABILITIES FOR TRENDING LOWER (SHORT)
content:
The more likely the market is trending and trending lower, the higher the probabilities that you're going to
get a nice swing trade on the short side.

section_title: DETERMINING MONTHLY PD ARRAYS AND ACTIVE PREMIUM ARRAYS (BEARISH)
content:
You're going to determine the monthly PD arrays and refer to the most active premium arrays. This means
asking: on a monthly chart, did we just recently move away from that point of origin?

section_title: IDENTIFYING THE FRAMEWORK FOR THE MONTHLY BEARISH MOVE
content:
Was that point of origin a bearish order block, an old high, or an old historical low that acted as resistance?
What was the framework that caused that move?

section_title: DETERMINING MONTHLY DISCOUNT ARRAYS AFTER PREMIUM RESISTANCE
content:
If the monthly premium arrays are resisting price, we then determine the discount arrays on the monthly
chart, meaning, where should price reach down to?

section_title: TRANSPOSING MONTHLY ACTIVE PREMIUM LEVELS TO WEEKLY CHART
content:
When the monthly premium arrays are active and defined on the monthly levels, we move those levels to
the weekly chart.

section_title: BREAKING DOWN WEEKLY PD ARRAYS ON A PREMIUM BASIS
content:
Then, we take the same approach and apply it to the weekly chart. We break down the PD arrays on a
premium basis and refer to all of the active premium arrays on the weekly chart.

section_title: DETERMINING WEEKLY DISCOUNT ARRAYS AFTER PREMIUM RESISTANCE
content:
If the weekly premium array is resisting price, we determine where the discount arrays are on the weekly
chart. So, we're defining our range in terms of premium to discount on the weekly as well.

section_title: ACTIVE WEEKLY PREMIUM ARRAY ELEMENTS AND TRANSPOSING TO DAILY
content:
When the weekly premium arrays are active and defined (such as bearish order blocks, bearish Breakers,
old highs, rejection blocks, any fair value gaps above you), that is transposed to the daily chart.

section_title: DETERMINING DAILY PREMIUM ARRAYS AND ACTIVE PREMIUM ARRAYS (BEARISH)
content:
Then you're going to determine the daily premium arrays, referring to the most active premium arrays on
the daily chart.

section_title: IDENTIFYING ACTIVE DAILY PREMIUM ARRAY ELEMENTS FOR SELLING
content:
This involves checking for:
- Bearish Breakers on the daily chart.
- Mitigation blocks above us on the daily chart.
- Fair value gaps or voids.
- Old highs.
- Candles that have long Wicks on them, focusing on piercing just above the bodies of the candles (a 'turtle
soup' idea).

section_title: TRANSPOSING ACTIVE DAILY PREMIUM ARRAYS TO FOUR-HOUR CHART
content:
When these daily premium arrays are active, you're going to take them and move them over to your four
- hour chart and transpose those.

section_title: DETERMINING FOUR-HOUR PREMIUM ARRAYS (BEARISH)
content:
Then you're going to do the same thing on your four-hour chart; you determine where the premium arrays
are, such as bearish order blocks, old highs, bearish Breakers, and mitigation blocks that are above you. All
those premium arrays.

section_title: FOCUSING ON ACTIVE FOUR-HOUR PREMIUM ARRAYS
content:
You're going to take these premium arrays and determine which occur or appear in the four-hour chart.
Then, you're going to refer to the active premium arrays for that time frame only.

section_title: IDENTIFYING ACTIVE FOUR-HOUR PREMIUM ARRAYS IN CURRENT MARKET
content:
You're going to only focus on the ones that are in the four-hour chart because there may not be a bearish
Breaker or any mitigation blocks in the current range that you're looking at on the four-hour chart. Whatever
ones that are active in your current market environment, that's what you take and label for your four-hour
chart.

section_title: EXECUTING SELLS ON FOUR-HOUR PREMIUM ARRAYS (1)
content:
You're going to sell every four-hour premium array. This means:
- Bearish order block.
- Bearish Breaker.
- Mitigation block.
- Trading back up into fair value, closing a range or fair value gap.

section_title: EXECUTING SELLS ON FOUR-HOUR PREMIUM ARRAYS (2)
content:
You'll sell short an old high, or a candle that's bold (which has a very short little Wick at the top), you'll look
for the price to trade through that high.

section_title: EXECUTING SELLS ON REJECTION BLOCKS (LONG WICKS)
content:
The key is you want to look for price not to go through the Wicks many times; that's the rejection block.
When you see long-wicked candles, and you're bearish, the price probably won't go above the Wicks many
times; it will just go above the body of the candles and then fail there. That's the rejection block.

section_title: SELLING ON TRANSPOSED LEVELS
content:
You'll sell all those on a four-hour basis, and any level that's been transposed from the daily, weekly, and
monthly as well.

section_title: TAKING PROFITS AT DISCOUNT ARRAYS FROM SHORT ENTRIES
content:
As you sell short, you're going to be looking to take profits at the daily, weekly, and monthly discount arrays.
In other words, all the bullish ideas that would support price as you reach them and price is moving in your
favor.

section_title: SCALING OUT AND MANAGING RISK AT LOWER TIMEFRAME TARGETS (SHORT)
content:
You're going to be scaling out as you get to those daily, weekly, and monthly discount arrays. The more you
get to the monthly and weekly discount range on those time frames, the less likely price will continue going
lower. You want to be at your smallest portion of your trades at that time; you don't want to be holding your
biggest portion then, because if price retraces or reverses altogether, you give back a lot of open profits,
and it's never fun.

section_title: IDEAL RISK-TO-REWARD RATIO FOR SETUPS (SHORT)
content:
You're going to look to trade setups that offer at least three, preferably more, times the range between your
entry and the closest discount array on all time frames.

section_title: FRAMING RISK-TO-REWARD RATIO (SHORT)
content:
You're basically framing your risk to reward to be at least for one dollar risk, you're trying to make three
dollars or more. Preferably, you only look for scenarios that have like five to one reward-to-risk.

section_title: INITIAL LOSS MANAGEMENT
content:
If a sell setup on the four-hour premium array fails and the trade results in a loss, all you have to do is don't
panic and accept the loss.

section_title: LOOKING FOR HIGHER-PRICED PREMIUM ARRAYS TO RESELL
content:
Now you're just going to simply look for the higher-priced four-hour premium array to sell at. For example,
you may have sold short at what you thought was a mitigation block, and it just ran right through you.

section_title: MOVING TO THE NEXT PREMIUM ARRAY FOR RESELLING
content:
Go to the next premium array and look to sell it again. We're focusing on monthly, weekly, and daily
conditions that are bearish. If you're executing on a four-hour chart and get stopped out on your initial trade
(not always, but sometimes), if that happens, you just simply go up to the next higher-priced premium array.

section_title: CONSIDERING ALL HIGHER-PRICED PREMIUM ARRAYS (OB, OLD HIGH)
content:
In other words, there may be a bearish order block it needs to trade to, or it may need to trade through an
old high. You take every single one of them.

section_title: REFERRING TO HIGHER TIMEFRAME PREMIUM ARRAYS ON 4H FAILURE
content:
If the four-hour chart has no more premium arrays to sell at, refer to the daily, weekly, and monthly
premium arrays to sell at a higher price than you currently have.

section_title: POSITION SIZING AND MANAGING LOSS RECOVERY (BEARISH)
content:
At the higher premium array, you're going to sell with 50 of the position size that you used from your initial
trade or previous failed trade that you utilized.

section_title: ACCEPTING PARTIAL LOSS RECOVERY AND MITIGATING LOSSES
content:
Do not try to win all the lost equity back in the subsequent trade. Get what you can get from the trade; don't
try to force it all back. If the trade only pans out and it slips to less probability, and you only recover 60 of
what you lost, be happy with the 60; at least you're mitigating the losses.

section_title: CONFIRMING PRICE ACTION DURING FAVORABLE MOVEMENT (BEARISH)
content:
Always confirm that lower time frame four-hour discount arrays give way or fail as price moves in your favor,
and new premium arrays resist price as it moves towards discount arrays on the higher time frame charts.

section_title: OBSERVING BULLISH DISCOUNT ARRAYS GIVING WAY (BEARISH CONFIRMATION)
content:
Basically, this means you want to be looking for bullish order blocks or down candles to be broken, and
every support level to be broken. You want to see new resistance levels found at old up candles.

section_title: NEW RESISTANCE AND INSTITUTIONAL SPONSORSHIP CONFIRMATION (BEARISH)
content:
As long as you keep seeing new resistance levels found at old up candles, you're seeing institutional
sponsorship on your side, and that's a great condition to be in when you're short.

section_title: CONCLUSION AND NEXT STEPS
content:
Now we're going to take a look at some specific setups that we can use with this idea for swing trading. We
will continue in Lesson Number Four.
